2000: Valparaiso LH brg SxE1/4E dist 20 mi.

There is a discrepancy between his time of departure in the columns and the Events.

People: 1730: At 5:30 PM F. A. Wardwell (O.S.) fell overboard from the Launch; immediately let go the life buoy, and cleared away the Life Boat and braced aback the Main Yards, took in studding sail, Mainsail and shanker. The man got hold of the Life buoy just on our quarter, and the 2nd Cutter in charge of Actg. Master's Mate W. H. Omey picked him up, safe and sound, also the life buoy. At 5:45 squared away again on our course.

Note: 1300: There is a change of course indicated, but the course doesn't change until 2100.

0000: From now to the end of the year, he enters his temperature data in the Attached Thermometer column. I kept all these values into the TA column. There is no explanation as to why he is doing this. I suspect these are really his dry bulb temperatures.  Also, for the 29th and 30th, he has values in the TT column. I suspect these are really TW values. The format of the log book has changed here, with more columns and with weather and events on separate pages. The previous logbooks had weather and events for one day on one page.
0800: All the TT values were entered in the TA column. I put then in the proper column.

Other: 0000: Heavens thickly studded with stars and frequently shooting in different directions.
0340: Observed two brilliant meteors to the Eastward.

Noon: Mare Island Navy Yard. 1200: These TT values of 74 for 1200 and 1400 beat the record of 72F for the Napa State Hospital for this date in 1943. Period of record 1893-2016. The Hospital is 10 miles N of the Navy Yard.
